item.title: China’s Ban on Meta-Manus Deal Shows Strains in U.S.-China Business Ties
item.description: China’s move to force Meta to unwind its acquisition of the AI startup demonstrates Beijing’s willingness to use economic weapons to safeguard key interests.

item.title: China Says Hostile Foreign Forces Are Driving Its Youth to Slack Off
item.description: The country’s intelligence agency blamed anti-China influences from abroad for stoking youth disillusionment.

item.title: Opinion | China’s AI ‘Hotel California’
item.description: Beijing kills Meta’s purchase of Manus in fear of talent flight.

item.title: When America and China Were Friendlier—and 10,000 Soldiers Built a Bike Track
item.description: Plus, a U.S. president’s unfavorable first impression of a lower-ranking Xi Jinping.

item.title: China Bans Meta’s Acquisition of Manus on National Security Grounds
item.description: Beijing has banned the $2.5 billion acquisition and ordered the parties involved to rescind the transaction.

item.title: China’s Industrial Profits Jump Despite Tighter Energy Markets
item.description: Industrial profit surged 15.8% in March despite tighter global energy markets due to the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Chinese Satellites Over Mideast Battlefield Put U.S. on Edge
item.description: Chinese satellite imagery of the conflict zone is potentially offering battlefield guidance to Iran and other U.S. adversaries.

item.title: As China Encroaches, Even New Zealand Is Getting Serious About Its Military
item.description: Billions will be spent in the coming years—including on missiles, drones and base upgrades—as the remote country squares up to a widening threat.

item.title: The U.S. Navy’s New Insurance Policy for War With China Is an Australian Base
item.description: The Pentagon plans to deploy submarines to an expanding facility in allies’ strategy to deter Beijing.

item.title: Chinese Satellites Over Mideast Battlefield Put U.S. on Edge
item.description: Chinese satellite imagery of the conflict zone is potentially offering battlefield guidance to Iran and other U.S. adversaries.
